Stephan Loewentheil photograph collection, 1822-1979.
Collection Number: 8043

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Stephan Loewentheil photograph collection, 1822-1979.
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
8043
Abstract:
Collection of photographs on various subjects demonstrating the technological evolution of photography, with emphasis on the period of 1822 to 1885. Includes 19th- and 20th-century photographs organized in several major groups.
Creator:
Loewentheil, Stephan
Stephan Loewentheil History of Photography Collection
Quanitities:
40.1 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
NOTES

Associated book collection cataloged separately.See the "Separated Material" section below for a list of the CDV albums which have also been cataloged individually.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Stephan Loewentheil Photograph Collection, #8043. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
RELATED MATERIALS

An on-line exhibit of "Dawn's Early Light" showcasing many items from this collection.
An on-line exhibit of "Lincoln's Unfinished Work" showcasing many of the Lincoln related items from this collection.
Scope and content

Collection of photographs on various subjects demonstrating the technological evolution of photography, with emphasis on the period of 1822 to 1885. Includes 19th- and 20th-century photographs organized in several major groups: Daguerre ephemera and early photographic processes, African American collection, Mathew Brady and the Civil War, Lincoln, Native Americans and the West, hand-colored photography, and cartes de visite. The collection contains daguerreotypes, salted paper photographs, ambrotypes, tintypes, albumen prints, cartes de visite, cabinet cards, stereographs, and photograph albums. Also included are lithographic prints (some hand colored), a graphite on paper portrait, wood engraving, medals, and stamp, as well as early photograph viewers such as a "hemisferium praxinoscope" and a "photographicon."
SEPARATED MATERIAL

Books - cataloged individually in the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

Salted paper prints
Viewers and early animation
MU 1512 MU-1512
Hemisferium praxinoscope
 Circa 1989-2010
Invented by Charles-Émile Reynaud in 1877, the praxinoscope was an animation device, the successor to the zoetrope. It used a strip of pictures placed around the inner surface of a spinning cylinder. An inner circle of mirrors was placed so that the reflections of the pictures appeared more or less stationary in position as the wheel turned. The viewer looking in the mirrors would thus see a rapid succession of images, producing the illusion of motion.
This modern praxinoscope is decorated on the top and outside with a circus theme and the designation "hemisferium." The pictures on the strips to be viewed include dancing figures, tumblers, and a toy soldier on a rocking horse.
Box I-1I-1 Folder 74
Harper's Weekly and The Daily Graphic articles featuring illustrations of horses
 1874-1887
Scope and Contents
Two illustrations from Harper's Weekly and one from The Daily Graphic depicting the way horses were traditionally drawn before inventions in photography allowed photographers to capture a horse in motion to illustrate the movement.

Exeter Engraving, British Association for the Advancement of Science souvenir booklet

Alexander Graham Bell inaugurating the New York to Chicago telephone line, Scientific American

Scope and Contents
Halftone reproduction of photograph in the November 5, 1892 issue of Scientific American.
Series II. Cartes de Visite
Scope and Contents
This series includes boxed CDVs. For the CDV albums that have been cataloged individually see the list at the end of this guide.
Single Images
Alphabetical by name and by subject

Cartes de visite viewers
MU 1511 MU-1511
Photographicon
 1865
Photograph viewer in the form of a wooden box covered in velvet, containing two rollers, with a viewing window on the top. By turning one of two knobs on the front of the device, the viewer can move forward or back through a roll of photographs on the two rollers. The roll contains many reproductions of engravings or lithographs in addition to (unidentified) portrait photographs. The images are in carte de visite format, but inserted into a continuous roll of paper photo frames. Images include a photo of a Union army officer from the Civil War.
Guide to the Loewentheil family collection of cartes-de-visite
Scope and Contents
The contents of this binder were created by the Loewentheil family in creating the collection. Sitter names and descriptions for many of the CDVs, both those individual and those bound in albums (listed under separated materials), are included. Locations may differ from current object locations within the collection.
Series III. Hand-colored Photographs
 1850s–1870s
Hand coloring.
Salted paper prints
Albumen prints.
Tintypes (prints).
Studio portraits.
Individual portraits
Box III-22III-3III-4III-5III-6III-10
Women
22 x 17 in and smaller
Scope and Contents
Contains a carte-de-visit of a Civil War era colorist at work. Most portraits are unidentified. Identified sitters include Sarah E. Marcy, Catherine Wentz Atwood, Mary Elizabeth Eastwood, Mrs. Frances Browning Marey, Sallie Fisher, Ann Mitchell, Mrs. Christian B. Mayer, Mrs. James P. Cook, Catherine B. Heron, Elizabeth Smiley Helligose, Mrs. George D'Almaine, Maria Parker Silkman, and Cecilia Grant. Photographers include Broadbent and Co., George A. Lenzf, G.M. Elton, Nathan H. DeVars, Walter Dinmore and Co., Erekson and Bodurtha, R. Newell, W.E. Christie, M. Ellsmoore, W.E. Entrekin, and H. Manger and Co.
Box III-3III-6III-7III-8III-10
Children
22 x 17 in and smaller
Scope and Contents
Contains portraits of children and infants, almost all unidentified. Several portraits are of children holding pet dogs. Photographers include J. Carroll McCaffrey, Jones and Brother, A.M. Burrough, D.A. Clifford, J.M. Horning, Erekson and Bodurtha, and L. Waite.
Box III-9III-13III-14III-15III-16III-10III-25
Men
22 x 17 in and smaller
Scope and Contents
Identified sitters include John Woodruff, Percival Roberts Sr., John Richards, Jeremiah Chapman (Box III-25) and Rev. J.G. Barton. All others unidentified. Photographers and artists include Charles Fredericks, J.A. Keenan, Irving M. Smith, H.R. Couch, R.A. Lewis, William S. Pendleton, Charles Cohill, Broadbent and Co., Elmira Portrait Co., J. Kolbeck, R.N. Keeley, L. Horning, J.H. Carpenter, Fred G. Milander, Terkelson and Henry Studios, Erekson and Bodurtha, and James Cremer.
Box III-17III-18
Civil War
 1861-1865
11 x 14 in and smaller
Scope and Contents
Includes portraits of soldiers, officers, and a hand-colored photograph of Abraham Lincoln, based on an original photograph by Anthony Berger. Other identified sitters include Major Hawksworth, Enoch W. Greene, Isaac DeYoung, Major Ivan Hicks, and L.A. Leighton. Most portraits are unidentified. Photographers include J.R. Hall, L. Horning, B.F. Reimer, and J. Kolbeck.
United State -- History -- Civil War, 1861-1865.
Soldiers--United States.
Military Uniforms

Double-sided portraits

Group portraits
Box III-19III-20III-24
Couples and companion portraits
11 x 14 in and smaller
Scope and Contents
Includes photographs of couples, and related photographs that are possibly companion photographs, meant to be displayed together. Portraits are mostly unidentified.

Series IV. African American Photographs
Processing Information
Many objects from this series have been digitized and are available in the Cornell Digital Collections portal.
African Americans.

African American men
Box IV-1IV-2IV-3IV-4IV-5IV-6IV-7IV-8IV-9IV-10IV-11IV-12IV-13IV-14IV-15IV-16IV-17IV-18
Family albums
Scope and Contents
Mostly unidentified family albums, contain photographs, postcards and correspondence.
Photograph albums
Portraits
Box IV-21IV-22IV-23
Framed portraits
 1900-1940
Box IV-19
Portrait in an oval button frame of young woman in a white dress holding flowers
Portrait mounted on a button frame illustrated with flowers.
Box IV-19
Portrait in an oval button frame of a woman
Button frame is illustrated with flowers.
Box IV-19
Portrait in an oval button frame of a young woman
Button frame is illustrated with flowers.
Box IV-19
Portrait in an oval button frame of a woman
Frame is illustrated with leaves and flowers.
Box IV-20
Portraits of Francis Warren and Annie Crawford in a round button frame
 1940s
Large button with photographs of two women. The background has a picture of an angel and reads "Peace, In Loving Remembrance of". The text at the bottom reads "Francis Warren, Born 1870, died Dec. 18, 1940" and "Annie Crawford, Born 1844, died Dec. 17, 1930"
Box IV-20
Portrait of a woman wearing a hat in a rectangular button frame
hand coloring
Box IV-20
Portrait of a man in a rectangular button frame
Frame is illustrated with a cabin and flowers.
Box IV-19
Button photograph of older woman
Mounted portrait of seated older woman. The back is a small mirror.
Box IV-19
Portraits of two women in a rectangular button frame
Hand-colored portraits of two African American women, affixed to frame
Box IV-19
Portrait of a woman holding a book, in a a rectangular button frame
Box IV-19
Portrait of Otis Lee Shepard in a rectangular button frame.
Photograph of young boy, captioned Otis Lee Shepard. Affixed to frame.
Box IV-19
Portrait of a man mounted on a pin
Button pin of an African American male, with US NA written on the side.
Box IV-19
Framed portrait of a soldier
 1940s
Framed portrait of a soldier with a gun, mounted to a rectangular button frame with a war-themed background. Portrait placed on a red flag with a blue star. Hand-colored
Box IV-19
Button portrait of woman
Photograph of woman standing outside, mounted to a small button
Box IV-20
Portrait of a man in an oval button frame
Frame, with illustrations of a "C", a trowel, and gavel, and a squared ruler.
Box IV-20
Portrait of seated woman in an oval button frame
Box IV-20
Portrait of a man and woman in a round button frame
Photograph affixed to a button of a woman with a towel and a man caricatured as a baby in a wash basin.
Box IV-20
Portrait of a baby in an oval button frame
hand coloring
Box IV-20
Portrait of woman in a fur shawl in a round button frame
Button frame is drawn as a peach. Hand-colored.
Box IV-20
Portrait of a woman in an oval button frame
Box IV-20
Portrait of man and woman in an oval button frame
Box IV-20
Portrait of a woman in a fur shawl in a round button frame
Hand-colored portrait of a woman in a fur shawl, affixed to a button frame. Colored roses and leaves in the background.
Box IV-20
Portrait of a man standing in the yard in a rectangular button frame
Hand-colored portrait of a young man wearing a suit coat, hat and tie in wooden frame.

United States -- History -- Civil War, 1961-1965
Historian George Sullivan's Mathew Brady Research Archive
Biographical / Historical
Historian George Sullivan, author of Mathew Brady: His Life and Photographs (1994) and In the Wake of Battle: The Civil War Images of Mathew Brady (2004) was one of the foremost authorities on the life and work of Mathew Brady. This collection, organized in three-ring binders and file folders, comprises correspondence, notes, web printouts, photocopies, photographic prints, and tear sheets, represents Sullivan's lifetime reference archive.

Hand coloring.

William Henry Seward's family CDV album
 Undated
Biographical / Historical
Seward was Lincoln's Secretary of State
Scope and Contents
The contents of the album have been housed separately in the same box.
Conditions Governing Use
Please use care when handling the album as the binding is broken.
Cartes-de-visite (card photographs).
Box VI-1
Cooper Union Address
 1860
Scope and Contents
First separate edition printed by the new York Tribune to promote Lincoln's presidential campaign.Original speach delivered at the Cooper Institute, Monday, February 27, 1860.
Box I-1 Folder 10
Charles A. Leale and family
 1880
creator: Pach Brothers (Firm)
45.7 x 56 cm
Scope and Contents
Charles A. Leale was the first physician to attend President Abraham Lincoln after Lincoln was shot at Ford's Theatre on April 14, 1865. This photograph shows the physician and his family at their home. Included are Dr. Leale, his wife Rebecca Medwin Copcutt Leale, and their children Annie, Lilian, Medwin, Marion, and Loyal.
Albumen prints.
Leale, Charles A. (Charles Augustus), 1842-1932
Box V-18
Lincoln, His Cabinet, and Civil War Officers - Engravings after Brady Photographs
 1860s

CDV album page with portraits of Abraham Lincoln, Mary Todd Lincoln, Lincoln and men associated with his presidency and assassination, and Confederate President Davis and his cabinet

Framed portraits of Lincoln

Four framed portraits of Lincoln, some in original frames.

Collage of portraits of the signers of the 13th Amendment
Framed photograph. Includes list of signer names and key to identifying portraits.
Box VI-9
Portrait of physician Charles Crane
Scope and Contents
Framed photograph of Charles Crane, one of the physicians attending Lincoln at the time of his death.

Series VIII. Edward Curtis: Native Americans and The West
 1925
Access to boxes VIII-1 through 4 restricted to permission of the curator.
Processing Information
Titles taken from Edward Curtis's The North American Indian.
Scope and Contents
These gelatin silver glass plate positives were used to make photogravure printing plates to illustrate Curtis's The North American Indian.
Indians of North America
Gelatin silver transparencies
